title: A Imprensa Brasileira e suas “Cruzadas Morais”: Análise dos Casos do Segundo Governo de Getúlio Vargas e do Primeiro Governo de Lula da Silva
fields: error_in_raw_text; is_empirical_quant_paper; paper_uses_survey_data; paper_uses_survey_data; evidence_type; evidence_type; method_status; method_status
brief_justification: Comparative analysis of media framing and moral crusades in Brazilian press (O Estado de S. Paulo, O Globo) during Vargas second government and Lula first government. Content analysis of editorials and news sections.

title: The Elusive New Middle Class in Brazil
fields: paper_uses_survey_data; evidence_type; method_status; single_region; effort_to_explore_mechanisms; main_variable_relationship
brief_justification: This is a descriptive empirical paper that uses survey microdata (POF 2008-2009) to characterize the socio-economic profile of Brazil's so-called 'new middle class' through consumption and living standard indicators. It does not estimate causal effects or seek determinants; rather, it challenges the classification of a particular income stratum as 'middle class' by showing their consumption patterns resemble vulnerable/poor strata more than traditional middle class. No regression models, causal claims, or identification strategies are employed -- the analysis is purely descriptive and classificatory.

title: O 18 Brumário, política e pós-modernismo
journal_title: Lua Nova: Revista de Cultura e Política
brief_justification: Theoretical essay analyzing interpretations of Marx's Eighteenth Brumaire. No empirical data, no quantitative methods. The paper is a textual/hermeneutic analysis of a classic political text, arguing against postmodern and heterodox readings by demonstrating the persistence of two materialist explanatory principles in the original work.

title: Perceptions on justice, the judiciary and democracy
brief_justification: This is a descriptive empirical paper that uses original survey data from four Brazilian metropolitan regions to explore citizens' perceptions of justice, trust in the judiciary, and dissatisfaction with democracy. It does not employ a causal research design or make causal claims; rather, it describes and explores attitudinal patterns regarding the judicialization of politics in Brazil. The analysis is based on survey responses but the abstract does not indicate regression modeling, causal inference techniques, or significance testing. The paper's goal is to argue descriptively that discontent with political institutions extends to the judiciary, undermining the normative basis for judicialization.

title: Benjamin Constant and the Combination of the Freedom of the Ancients with that of the Moderns
brief_justification: This is a political theory article that performs an interpretive reading of Benjamin Constant's writings on ancient and modern liberty. It engages exclusively with textual and philosophical analysis of primary sources and secondary literature in the republican and liberal traditions. There are no data, no quantitative methods, no empirical claims, and no causal inferences. The article's contribution is entirely normative and hermeneutic.

title: Vinte anos da Colonialidade do Poder, Eurocentrismo e América Latina de Aníbal Quijano
brief_justification: This is a purely theoretical forum article comprising three essays that critically discuss Aníbal Quijano's concept of 'Coloniality of Power' on its twentieth anniversary. It contains no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, no empirical methods, and no causal claims. The article engages in intellectual history, interdisciplinary reflection, and macro-historical sociology from a conceptual perspective.

title: Hegemonia, criação de consenso e colonização pedagógica: Novas formas de ingerência externa?
brief_justification: This is a theoretical paper grounded in Neo-Gramscian Critical Theory that examines US hegemony and consensus fabrication in Latin America through the Atlas Network. It relies on primary documents, public records (e.g., Atlas Network Form 990 filings), and specialized bibliography. It contains no quantitative analysis, no statistical tables, no figures, and no equations. The paper is fundamentally a qualitative-theoretical contribution to International Relations, not an empirical quantitative study.

title: Entre a Desinstitucionalização e a Resiliência: Participação Institucional no Governo Bolsonaro
brief_justification: Mixed-methods study of 103 Brazilian federal participatory councils under Bolsonaro (2019-2022). Uses original legal/administrative dataset to classify councils by normative and activity status across policy areas. Develops a typology of institutional resilience based on design strength and policy community insertion. Primarily descriptive/typological with qualitative comparative logic rather than formal quantitative causal inference. Implicit causal claims about government preferences and institutional design driving differential deinstitutionalization, but no regression models, statistical tests, or formal causal design.

title: O local e o global na estrutura da política ambiental internacional: a construção social do acidente químico ampliado de Bhopal e da Convenção 174 da OIT
brief_justification: This is a qualitative constructivist International Relations paper that analyzes the social construction of the Bhopal chemical disaster and its normative impact leading to ILO Convention 174. It employs no quantitative methods, datasets, or statistical analysis. The paper uses theoretical interpretation (constructivism, co-constitution of agents and structures) and historical/documentary analysis of international environmental norms, conferences (Stockholm 1972, Rio 1992), and the role of ILO. It is a single-case interpretive study in the IR subfield of International Environmental Politics.

title: Bobbio na história das idéias democráticas
journal_title: Lua Nova: Revista de Cultura e Política
brief_justification: This is a political theory article that analyzes Norberto Bobbio's intellectual trajectory through close reading of his texts and secondary literature. It traces his shift from ethical-participatory democracy (1940s actionist period) to procedural democracy (post-1955), situating both within broader traditions of democratic thought. No empirical data, no quantitative methods, and no causal claims are present. The method is interpretive analysis of published works and intellectual history.

title: Relevant Factors for the Voting Decision in the 2002 Presidential Election: An Analysis of the ESEB (Brazilian Electoral Study) Data
brief_justification: This is a quantitative electoral behavior study of the 2002 Brazilian presidential election using post-electoral survey data (ESEB) and logistic regression models. The paper tests hypotheses about factors driving vote choice (religion, partisanship, ideology, government evaluation, candidate attributes) for four candidates. It uses a kitchen-sink logistic regression approach with multiple blocks of predictors rather than a causal identification strategy. The language uses 'explain' in quotes, suggesting awareness that the associations are not strictly causal, but the framing of 'relevant factors for the voting decision' and 'determinants' implies causal reasoning without formal identification.

title: As teorias dos movimentos sociais: um balanço do debate
journal_title: Lua Nova: Revista de Cultura e Política
brief_justification: Purely theoretical literature review article that surveys and compares three main paradigms of social movement theory (Resource Mobilization, Political Process, New Social Movements) and their subsequent reformulations. Contains no empirical data collection or quantitative analysis.

title: Welfare state e desenvolvimento
brief_justification: The paper is a theoretical-normative literature review that systematizes arguments and evidence on the relationship between the welfare state and economic development. It contains no tables, figures, or equations, has 38 references across 28 pages, and its abstract frames the goal as marshaling arguments against common assumptions rather than testing hypotheses with original data. The method is essayistic, relying on conceptual argumentation and synthesis of existing studies rather than explicit empirical methodology.

title: BRICS: an explanation in critical geography
brief_justification: This is a purely theoretical/conceptual paper that uses Critical Geography and Critical Geopolitics literature to interpret the BRICS grouping through three conceptual tools (Space, Geographical and Geopolitical Imaginations, and Region). It contains no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, no tables, figures, or equations. It is a conceptual essay in International Relations, not an empirical study.

title: A institucionalização de blocos de integração: uma proposta de critérios de medição
brief_justification: This is a conceptual and comparative-qualitative paper that proposes measurement criteria for the institutionalization of regional integration blocs. It applies these criteria descriptively to four integration processes (CAN, MERCOSUR, UNASUR, EU) without any statistical or quantitative empirical analysis. There is no regression, no causal inference design, no survey data, and no hypothesis testing. The paper is a framework-building exercise in comparative regionalism.

title: Brazilian Congress, 2014 elections and governability challenges
brief_justification: This is a research note that critically examines claims about the 2014 Brazilian congressional elections, discussing parliamentary fragmentation and ideological positioning. It is a qualitative/descriptive analysis of election outcomes and governability challenges under coalition presidentialism, without statistical modeling, hypothesis testing, or quantitative causal inference methods.

title: Paradiplomacia, Políticas de Segurança e Redes de Cidades: o Caso da Unidade Temática de Segurança Cidadã das Mercocidades
brief_justification: This is a qualitative case study in International Relations examining paradiplomacy and policy transfer/diffusion of citizen security policies through the Mercocities network in South America. The paper contains no tables, figures, or equations, relies on documentary sources and institutional reports, and does not employ any quantitative or statistical methods. It is a descriptive-analytical piece grounded in policy transfer and paradiplomacy frameworks rather than causal inference or quantitative empirical analysis.

title: Sobre a Espectralidade do Inter-estado-ual/Internacional: Um Fórum sobre os Espectros de Marx de Jacques Derrida depois de 25 anos, Parte III
brief_justification: This is a purely theoretical forum comprising three essays on Jacques Derrida's Specters of Marx, exploring concepts of spectrality, hospitality, sovereignty, and the international through post-structuralist and deconstructive lenses. It contains no quantitative data, no empirical analysis, and no causal claims. The paper belongs to International Relations theory and political philosophy.

title: Liberalização da conta financeira e seus efeitos sobre crescimento econômico e instabilidade financeira: os modelos ortodoxos exante estavam errados
brief_justification: This is a literature review article that synthesizes and critiques existing mainstream empirical studies on the effects of capital account liberalization on economic growth and financial instability. It does not conduct any original quantitative analysis — it contains zero tables, figures, or equations, and only 25 references across 20 pages. The article argues narratively that orthodox ex ante theoretical models underpinning financial liberalization in the 1980s-1990s were contradicted by subsequent empirical evidence. As a review essay without original data analysis or formal methodology, it is classified as essayistic and qualitative.

title: Política Externa dos EUA para a América do Sul desde o 11 de Setembro: Negligência ou Militarização?
brief_justification: This is a qualitative International Relations paper that analyzes US foreign policy toward South America after 9/11 using descriptive data on military/economic assistance, arms transfers, SOUTHCOM posture statements, official documents, and diplomatic cables. It does not employ any quantitative statistical methods, regression models, or formal causal identification strategies. The paper makes implicit causal claims (e.g., Latin American assertiveness was caused by leftist governments and external power involvement, not US neglect) and explores mechanisms through historical and documentary analysis, but without quantitative empirical methods.

title: Produtividade, investimento e fluxo de capital: o fracasso do crescimento com poupança externa no Brasil
brief_justification: Empirical macroeconomics paper using time-series data for Brazil with 2SLS and GMM estimators to address endogeneity between productivity and investment/wage variables. Tests a new-developmentalist hypothesis that foreign savings (capital flow) does not promote productive investment. The paper specifies econometric equations, acknowledges simultaneity bias, and uses instrumental variable methods, though the specific instruments and sample size are not detailed in the available metadata. Published in a political economy journal with a Kaldorian/post-Keynesian theoretical framework.

title: 'Novas direções para a Análise de Política Externa': reconfigurando o campo
brief_justification: This is an editorial introduction to a special issue on new directions for Foreign Policy Analysis. It is a purely theoretical and programmatic essay that discusses interpretivist and post-positivist approaches to FPA and Diplomatic Studies, with no empirical quantitative analysis, no dataset, no statistical methods, and no causal claims. It frames contributions to the special issue and argues for methodological pluralism in FPA.

title: The African Agenda of Evangelical Christians in Brazilian Foreign Policy: The Crisis of the Universal Church of the Kingdom of God in Angola
brief_justification: This is a qualitative case study analyzing how Evangelical Christian groups, particularly the Universal Church of the Kingdom of God (IURD), influenced Brazilian foreign policy toward Africa during the Bolsonaro government. The paper uses the 2019 IURD crisis in Angola as a single case study. It contains no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, no tables or figures, and relies on documentary sources including news reports and secondary literature. The paper implicitly argues that Evangelicals shaped foreign policy but does not employ any formal causal research design or quantitative methods.

title: José Maria Ibiapina, um Ultramontano Heterodoxo?*
brief_justification: Historical-interpretive article analyzing primary sources (19th-century newspapers, episcopal texts, missionary chronicles) and secondary historiography to reassess Father Ibiapina's relationship to ultramontane Catholic reform in Imperial Brazil. No quantitative data or statistical analysis is employed; the method is archival/documentary historical analysis.

title: O Jovem Claude Lefort entre Socialismo e Democracia: Uma Leitura Sociológica*
brief_justification: Theoretical and historical article using a sociological reading of Claude Lefort's intellectual trajectory and writings from the 1940s-1960s. No quantitative data, statistical analysis, or empirical dataset. The paper relies on documentary analysis of Lefort's published texts, journals (Socialisme ou Barbarie, Les Temps Modernes), and secondary literature on French intellectual history.

title: O conceito de federalismo e a idéia de interesse no Brasil do século XIX
brief_justification: This is a conceptual history (Begriffsgeschichte) article using Koselleck's methodology to trace the evolution of the concept of federalism in 19th century Brazil. It analyzes newspapers and parliamentary debates as primary sources but employs no quantitative methods. The work is interpretive and historical-theoretical, focused on political concepts and their semantic transformations rather than empirical hypothesis testing.

title: Ruth Corrêa Leite Cardoso
brief_justification: This is a 4-page obituary/tribute to Ruth Corrêa Leite Cardoso by Gilberto Velho, published in Dados (2008). It is not a research article; it is a memorial note summarizing her intellectual trajectory and contributions to anthropology and political science. No research question, methodology, data, or analysis is present. Classification fields related to empirical or theoretical content are set to null or false.

title: A revolução russa e o sistema internacional
journal_title: Lua Nova: Revista de Cultura e Política
brief_justification: Historical-interpretive essay analyzing the Russian Revolution's insertion into the international system through Kissinger's theoretical framework. No quantitative data, no empirical design. Relies on documentary/archival historical interpretation with only two bibliographic references. Pure qualitative/theoretical analysis in international relations.

title: Construção democrática e modelos de Constituição
brief_justification: This is a theoretical-normative article that proposes a conceptual framework for analyzing constitutional models in new democracies. It discusses constitutionalism, policy-oriented constitutions, veto points, amendment processes, and judicial review without any quantitative empirical analysis. There are zero tables, figures, and equations. The 62 references are predominantly theoretical works on constitutionalism and democratic theory. The method is essayistic, relying on conceptual argumentation rather than systematic empirical evidence.

title: Os alunos do ensino médio e Sciences Po: entre a meritocracia e a percepção das desigualdades
brief_justification: Qualitative sociological study using ethnographic fieldwork in four high schools in Seine-Saint-Denis to analyze how Sciences Po outreach programs affect students' perceptions of meritocracy and inequality. No quantitative analysis, no statistical models, no causal inference design. The article draws on the French sociology of education tradition (Dubet, Van Zanten, Oberti) and employs qualitative methods to examine reception mechanisms of an unprecedented educational offer in stigmatized urban environments.

title: O Irã e atores religiosos xiitas transnacionais: Limites da influência política
brief_justification: This is a qualitative case study in international relations examining Iran's transnational Shia networks and their limited political influence in Iraq. The paper uses no quantitative data, no tables, no figures, and no statistical analysis. It develops the concept of 'transnational religious soft power' and applies it to the Iran-Iraq case, relying on secondary sources and analytical argumentation rather than systematic empirical methods.

title: Continuidade, Ruptura ou Reciclagem? Uma Análise do Programa Político do Banco Mundial após o Consenso de Washington
brief_justification: This is a theoretical-analytical essay that traces the evolution of the World Bank's political program from the Washington Consensus era (1989) through 2014. It relies on documentary analysis of the Bank's own publications and secondary literature, with no quantitative data, statistical analysis, or empirical testing. The article argues the Bank's agenda was 'recycled' rather than fundamentally changed, incorporating neo-institutionalist ideas to expand its liberal reform prescriptions across economy, state, civil society, and individual subjectivity in client countries. There are zero tables, figures, or equations (confirmed by counts in XML metadata). The 86 references are predominantly books and critical political economy journals. The method is interpretive-historical and essayistic throughout.

title: Domestic coalitions in the FTAA negotiations: the Brazilian case
brief_justification: This is a qualitative single-country case study of domestic coalitions in Brazil during FTAA negotiations. It uses historical and process-tracing analysis drawing on political economy theories (Rogowski, Katzenstein, Milner) to explain why industrialists and trade unions failed to align despite convergent trade policy preferences. The paper contains no quantitative data analysis, no tables, no figures, and no equations. It makes an implicit causal argument that trade liberalization altered the priorities of labor and capital, preventing coalition formation, but does so through qualitative narrative rather than statistical methods.

title: O Seguro Social e a Construção da Proteção do Trabalho no Brasil
brief_justification: Historical-analytical study of social insurance and labor protection in Brazil during the Vargas era (1930s-1940s). Uses archival and documentary sources (legislation, government magazines like Cultura Politica, secondary historiography) to argue that state elites deliberately deployed social insurance benefits to strengthen union organization and expand social legislation. No quantitative data, no tables, no figures, no equations. The method is essayistic historical analysis grounded in primary documents.

title: Uma análise da regra de ouro no Brasil
brief_justification: This is a descriptive institutional analysis of Brazil's fiscal golden rule. The paper explains how the rule operates and evaluates it against international experience (UK, Germany). It contains no tables, figures, or equations, no statistical analysis, no regression models, and only 14 references across 12 pages. It is an essayistic policy evaluation piece, not a quantitative empirical study.

title: Por uma Abordagem Interdisciplinar, Estrutural e Interseccional de Usuárias(os) do Estado: Comentário Crítico ao Artigo “Categorizando Usuários ‘Fáceis’ e ‘Difíceis’...” de Gabriela Spanghero Lotta e Roberto Rocha Coelho Pires
brief_justification: This is a critical commentary (Comentário Crítico) on another article, not an independent empirical study. It proposes a theoretical framework — interdisciplinary, structural, and intersectional — for analyzing state-governed populations and public policy beneficiaries. It contains no data analysis, no tables, no figures, no equations, and only 14 references, consistent with a short commentary piece rather than an empirical or even qualitative research article.

title: Social Scientists and Public Administration in the Lula da Silva Government
brief_justification: This article examines the presence of social scientists in high-level federal government positions during the Lula da Silva administration in Brazil. While the authors mention 'empirical research' in the abstract, the paper is fundamentally a descriptive, qualitative study of elite composition and academic backgrounds in public administration. It does not employ quantitative statistical methods, regression models, or causal inference designs. The hypothesis offered — that social scientists' presence is explained more by links to public service than by disciplinary knowledge — is framed discursively rather than tested with formal quantitative methods. The paper fits Public Policy/Administration given its focus on bureaucratic elites and government staffing.

title: O contextualismo linguístico na história do pensamento político: Quentin Skinner e o debate metodológico contemporâneo
brief_justification: This is a purely theoretical-normative essay on the methodology of the history of political thought. It critically reviews Quentin Skinner's linguistic contextualism and the objections raised by his critics (epistemological, intentionalist, and antiquarianist). There is no empirical data, no quantitative analysis, and no causal claims. The method is essayistic, relying on interpretive analysis of scholarly arguments and texts.

title: Memória e historicidade em dois "comandos" prisionais
journal_title: Lua Nova: Revista de Cultura e Política
brief_justification: This is a qualitative anthropological study that constructs a fictional dialogue between prisoners affiliated with two São Paulo prison commands (PCC and CRBC) to map different historicities and memories about prison 'wars'. It draws on ethnographic fieldwork and uses no quantitative methods, statistical analysis, or causal inference. The authors employ ethnographic/participant observation methodology grounded in anthropological theory (Lévi-Strauss, Viveiros de Castro, Deleuze & Guattari) to explore competing narratives and viewpoints among prisoners.

title: Globalização e religião: Efeitos do pluralismo global no campo religioso contemporâneo
brief_justification: This is a theoretical essay in the sociology of religion that discusses the effects of globalization on religious pluralization and identity autonomy. It analyzes two illustrative cases but employs no quantitative methods, no statistical analysis, no formal research design, and no systematic data collection. All 35 references are books. The article is structured as a conceptual discussion drawing on sociological theory (Giddens, Bauman, Berger, Hervieu-Léger) rather than empirical testing of hypotheses.

title: Narrar – mostrar – ver: Como analisar um álbum de fotos de família em conexão com uma entrevista narrativa
brief_justification: This is a sociology article (not political science) presenting a qualitative methodology for analyzing family photo albums in connection with narrative biographical interviews. The author, based at the University of Vienna's Institute of Sociology, demonstrates a method of pictorial interpretation (segment analysis) applied to a single family album case study. There is no quantitative analysis, no causal claims, no statistical methods, and no focus on political science topics. The paper is methodological in nature, falling outside standard political science subfields.

title: Organizar a Desordem: Raízes do Brasil em 1936
brief_justification: This is a work of Brazilian political thought/intellectual history. The author reconstructs the arguments of Sergio Buarque de Holanda's Raízes do Brasil (1936), tracing his intellectual dialogue with Oliveira Vianna and Gilberto Freyre around concepts of cordiality, political doctrines vs. national reality, and the idea of 'organizing disorder.' There is no quantitative data, no empirical hypothesis testing, and no causal research design. The method is interpretive-hermeneutic essay based on close reading of primary texts.

title: Governos Lula e Dilma em matéria de seguridade social e acesso à educação superior
brief_justification: This article provides a qualitative policy review ('balanço') of social policies under the Lula and Dilma governments (2003-2016), covering Bolsa Família, social security, minimum wage, health (SUS), and higher education access. It contains no tables, figures, or equations, and employs no quantitative methods, statistical analysis, or causal research design. The analysis is essayistic, drawing on secondary sources and policy documents to argue that these governments improved conditions for the poorest via income transfers while leaving structural causes of poverty unchanged and consolidating private sector roles in social policy.

title: A renda básica de cidadania e Quênia
brief_justification: The article is an essayistic, advocacy-oriented piece describing the history and evolution of Universal Basic Income legislation in Brazil and positive experiences in Kenya and Maricá (RJ). It contains no tables, figures, equations, or quantitative analysis. It is primarily normative and descriptive, arguing in favor of UBI adoption, and does not employ any systematic empirical research design.

title: Alan Greenspan, the confidence strategy
brief_justification: This article is an essayistic, theoretical analysis of Alan Greenspan's monetary policy strategy ('confidence strategy') contrasted with the mainstream 'credibility strategy.' Published in the Brazilian Journal of Political Economy, it belongs to political economy/macroeconomic policy rather than a core political science subfield. It does not conduct any empirical quantitative analysis, uses no data or formal methods, and relies entirely on interpretive discussion of Greenspan's speeches, testimonies, and secondary literature.

title: The contagion effect of public debt on monetary policy: the Brazilian experience
brief_justification: This is a short theoretical/analytical paper in political economy (published in the Brazilian Journal of Political Economy) that proposes an explanation for why Brazil's inter-bank interest rate is high relative to other emerging economies, arguing that the interplay between bank reserves and government securities markets transmits the public debt risk premium into the inter-bank rate. It contains no empirical data analysis, no figures, tables, or equations, has only 2 references and 8 pages, and reads as an essayistic argument rather than a formal empirical or methodological study. The subfield is classified as 'Other' because it falls under political economy/macroeconomics rather than a standard political science subfield.

title: Liquidez internacional e ciclo reflexo: algumas observações para a América Latina
brief_justification: This is a theoretical paper published in the Brazilian Journal of Political Economy that applies Minsky's financial fragility hypothesis to analyze reflex cycles in peripheral Latin American economies (Brazil, Argentina, Mexico) driven by international liquidity. It is primarily a theoretical-analytical piece grounded in Post-Keynesian economics, with no explicit empirical methodology, quantitative data analysis, or formal research design -- it uses theoretical frameworks to describe and interpret macroeconomic phenomena rather than conducting original empirical analysis. The subfield is classified as 'Other' because it is a political economy / macroeconomics paper rather than fitting neatly into standard political science subfields.

title: Transnational Social Movements and the Globalization Agenda: A Methodological Approach Based on the Analysis of the World Social Forum
brief_justification: This is a theoretical paper that proposes a conceptual and methodological framework for analyzing transnational social movements, using the World Social Forum as an illustrative case. It contains no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, no tables or figures, and relies entirely on theoretical argumentation drawing from political theory (Habermas, Rawls, Arendt, Touraine) and social movement literature. The paper discusses globalization as a political and cultural struggle and argues that transnational social movements represent a new form of political subject. It fits International Relations given its focus on transnational politics and world governance beyond nation-states.

title: The United Nations System and the Process of Political Articulation of Local Authorities as a Global Actor
brief_justification: This article analyzes the interaction between local authorities (organized as UCLG) and the United Nations system, examining how subnational governments achieved political objectives despite limited power resources. It is a qualitative, descriptive-analytical piece grounded in International Relations theory (transnational actors, paradiplomacy, norm creation literature). There is no quantitative data, no statistical analysis, and no formal research design. The paper uses a theoretical framework of 'mixed actors' (sovereignty-bound and sovereignty-free) to explain the political articulation of local authorities as global actors. It is essayistic in method, relying on documentary analysis and theoretical argumentation rather than systematic empirical methods.

title: Discutindo o papel do Programa Bolsa Família na decisão das eleições presidenciais brasileiras de 2006
brief_justification: This paper investigates the causal effect of the Bolsa Família program on Lula's vote share in the 2006 presidential election using municipal-level data from MDS, IBGE, and TSE. The authors run a regression controlling for city characteristics (urban/rural, population size, etc.) and claim the program was responsible for 45% of Lula's total votes, making an explicit causal claim without formal identification strategy. The analysis uses publicly available administrative data aggregated at the municipality level, not survey data, and the research design is a standard linear regression with controls (kitchen sink approach).

title: Ciência econômica e modelos de explicação científica: retomando a questão
brief_justification: This is a theoretical essay on scientific explanation models in economics, discussing methodological issues from J.S. Mill through Friedman to complexity science, using the taxonomy of scientific explanations from modern positivism as its analytical framework. It contains no empirical data analysis, no tables, figures, or equations (all counts are zero), and is purely a philosophy-of-science/methodology discussion. Published in the Brazilian Journal of Political Economy, it falls under methodology and formal theory with an essayistic approach.

title: Private Security and the State in Latin America: the Case of Mexico City
brief_justification: This is a qualitative, single-country case study of the private security market in Mexico City. It draws on regulatory documents, media reports, and secondary literature to describe how the local state and its public security agencies shape private security provision through both formal and informal practices. There is no quantitative analysis, no dataset, no statistical methods, and no causal identification strategy. The article is descriptive and analytical rather than empirical-quantitative.

title: Government, political actors and governance in urban policies in Brazil and São Paulo: concepts for a future research agenda
brief_justification: This is an analytical essay that critically reviews the concept of governance in Latin America and Brazil, proposes an operational definition of governance for studying urban policies, and discusses relevant political actors. It contains no quantitative analysis, no data, no tables or figures, and explicitly self-identifies as an 'analytical essay.' The goal is conceptual clarification and agenda-setting for future research, not empirical testing or causal inference.

title: When is statistical significance not significant?
brief_justification: This is a methodological paper that introduces the p-value statistic and demonstrates, through simulations, replications, and observational data, when statistical significance is not meaningful. It argues that graphical analysis should precede p-value interpretation, p-values are pointless for non-random samples and population data, and are highly sensitive to sample size. It does not test a substantive political science hypothesis and has no causal research design. It references Cohen's power analysis book, hence the power analysis flag. The evidence is quantitative (simulations and data demonstrations) with explicit methodology.

title: Memória de idosos: As narrativas em diferentes espaços de interação social
brief_justification: This is a qualitative sociology/anthropology article analyzing memory narratives of elderly residents in Santo André (SP, Brazil) who participated in a university extension project involving digital inclusion. It uses oral history and narrative analysis methods drawing on Halbwachs, Bosi, Pollak, Goffman, and Bourdieu. There is no quantitative component, no causal inference framework, and no statistical analysis. The article falls outside political science subfields, belonging more to social sciences/sociology/gerontology.

title: Non-domination and Political Institutions: The Contested Concept of Republican Democracy
brief_justification: This is a theoretical-normative article in political theory that compares three neo-Roman republican models of democracy (Pettit, Bellamy, McCormick) to argue that the concept of republican democracy is essentially contested. It contains no empirical data, no quantitative analysis, and no causal claims. The method is essayistic, relying on conceptual analysis and comparison of normative theories.

title: Planejamento estatal e convenções de desenvolvimento para a transição verde sustentável
brief_justification: This is a theoretical-normative essay arguing for the role of state planning in promoting a sustainable green transition through a 'Sustainable Development Convention.' It draws on Keynesian and institutional economics to make a normative case for the developmental State versus the neoliberal State. There is no empirical quantitative analysis: zero tables, figures, or equations, and no data or statistical methods are employed.
